architectureNotes: This agent is one of four built concurrently in separate repo clones. All inter-agent communication goes through SQLite only — never direct Python calls between agents. This branch reads from prospect_briefs (written by feature/agent-research) and writes to qualification_briefs. adela_outbound/db/schemas.py and adela_outbound/db/contracts.py are locked and owned by feature/agent-discovery — never modify them. This is the first agent with a LangGraph HITL interrupt — every qualification decision must come from Justin via the API, never automatically. Routers must be self-contained for a single include_router() merge. No module-level side effects. Business logic in graph nodes only. Full rules are in CLAUDE.md which Ralph reads before this file on every iteration.
description: ICP Qualification Agent — scores each researched company against the Adela ICP definition using Claude Sonnet, produces a per-criterion QualificationBrief, then pauses via LangGraph interrupt() for Justin's approval or rejection. Rejection notes are mandatory and feed a weekly ICP refinement loop. Approved companies move to status='qualified' in discovery_queue for pickup by the Drafting Agent. This branch runs concurrently with feature/agent-discovery, feature/agent-research, and feature/agent-drafting in separate repo clones.

parallelContext.prerequisite: feature/agent-discovery must have completed US-001 and pushed to remote. Cherry-pick that commit before starting: git fetch origin && git cherry-pick <US-001-commit-hash>. Verify: python3 -c 'from adela_outbound.db.contracts import QualificationBrief, ICPCriterion, ICPDefinition; print("OK")'
parallelContext.inputFrom: Reads ProspectBrief rows from prospect_briefs table where status is implicitly available via company_id. Reads discovery_queue for company metadata. Reads icp_definition table for current ICP criteria.
parallelContext.outputTo: Writes QualificationBrief rows to qualification_briefs table. Updates discovery_queue.status to 'qualified' (approved) or 'disqualified' (rejected). Writes icp_feedback rows on rejection. Writes icp_suggestions rows from weekly job.
parallelContext.mainPyInstruction: adela_outbound/api/main.py is owned by feature/agent-discovery. Do not modify it. It contains a commented placeholder for the qualification router. Leave it commented — the merge coordinator uncomments it.

techStack.language: Python 3.9.6+. Use from __future__ import annotations at top of all files using Optional[] type hints. No match/case, no X|None union syntax, no ParamSpec.
techStack.agentOrchestration: LangGraph 0.2+. StateGraph with TypedDict state. THIS AGENT USES HITL INTERRUPT — compile with MemorySaver checkpointer and interrupt_before=['hitl_gate']. Resume with Command(resume=payload).
techStack.database: SQLite via aiosqlite. Always use get_db() from adela_outbound.db.connection.
techStack.llm: anthropic Python SDK. AsyncAnthropic client. Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514.
techStack.hitlPattern: LangGraph HITL uses: (1) compile graph with checkpointer=MemorySaver() and interrupt_before=['hitl_gate']. (2) invoke with config={configurable: {thread_id: company_id}}. (3) graph pauses at hitl_gate node. (4) resume with graph.ainvoke(Command(resume=decision_payload), config={configurable: {thread_id: company_id}}). The thread_id is the company_id — each company has its own checkpoint.
techStack.confirmedMockPattern: AsyncMock for all async mocks. httpx: MockClient.return_value.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_instance); __aexit__ = AsyncMock(return_value=False). AsyncAnthropic: patch class, set return_value to mock_client, then mock_client.messages.create = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_response).

icpSeedDefinition.description: These are the 8 seed ICP criteria stored in the icp_definition table on first startup. They are editable from the dashboard. The qualification agent always reads from the DB — never from hardcoded values after seeding.

criteria.name: Services company in infrastructure mode
criteria.description: Has crossed from project work into infrastructure mode — deploying the same core product across 5+ enterprise clients

criteria.name: Regulated industry clients
criteria.description: Serves clients in financial services, healthcare, government, or other regulated verticals where per-client context governance is non-negotiable

criteria.name: AI or agent-heavy deployment
criteria.description: Core offering involves deploying AI agents or bespoke technical work into enterprise environments

criteria.name: Team size 5-50
criteria.description: Large enough to feel scaling pain, small enough to not have built internal tooling yet

criteria.name: Pre-seed through Series B
criteria.description: Later stage companies have budget but also internal tooling capacity. Earlier stage may not yet feel the pain.

criteria.name: FDE or solutions engineer role signal
criteria.description: Has forward deployed engineer, solutions engineer, or deployment engineer titles — explicit signal they are in the problem space

criteria.name: Recent scaling signal
criteria.description: Growing headcount, new funding, expanding client list — signal they are in the scaling pain window right now

criteria.name: Technical founding team
criteria.description: Founding team has engineering background — more likely to appreciate and evaluate infrastructure tooling seriously

icpSeedDefinition.autoDisqualifyRule: Companies with fit_tier = 'disqualified' (fit_score < 0.30) are auto-rejected without entering the HITL queue. They are written directly to qualification_briefs with status='auto_rejected' and discovery_queue.status updated to 'disqualified'. Justin only reviews weak, moderate, and strong.

userStories.title: ICP seed definition and database operations
userStories.description: Implement icp.py with the seed ICP criteria, the seed-on-startup function, and the ICP loader. The ICP definition is stored in SQLite and editable from the dashboard — the qualification agent always reads from the DB, never from hardcoded values at runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: adela_outbound/agents/qualification/icp.py — replace stub with full implementation.

Imports: from __future__ import annotations; import logging; import json; import uuid; from datetime import datetime, timezone; from adela_outbound.db.connection import get_db; logger = logging.getLogger(__name__).

SEED_ICP_CRITERIA module-level list of dicts — exactly 8 items matching icpSeedDefinition.criteria in this PRD. Each dict has keys: id (str), name (str), description (str), weight (str — exactly one of 'high', 'medium', 'low'). Example first item: {'id': 'services_mode', 'name': 'Services company in infrastructure mode', 'description': 'Has crossed from project work into infrastructure mode — deploying the same core product across 5+ enterprise clients', 'weight': 'high'}. Copy all 8 from icpSeedDefinition.criteria exactly.

async def seed_icp_if_empty(conn) -> None: cursor = await conn.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM icp_definition'); row = await cursor.fetchone(); count = row[0] if row else 0; if count > 0: logger.debug('ICP definition already seeded, skipping'); return. Insert seed: seed_id = str(uuid.uuid4()); now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(); await conn.execute('INSERT INTO icp_definition (id, version, criteria, created_at) VALUES (?,?,?,?)', (seed_id, 1, json.dumps(SEED_ICP_CRITERIA), now)); await conn.commit(); logger.info(f'Seeded ICP definition with {len(SEED_ICP_CRITERIA)} criteria').

async def load_icp(conn) -> dict: cursor = await conn.execute('SELECT * FROM icp_definition ORDER BY version DESC LIMIT 1'); row = await cursor.fetchone(); if not row: raise RuntimeError('ICP definition not found — seed_icp_if_empty must be called on startup'); d = dict(row); d['criteria'] = json.loads(d.get('criteria', '[]')); return d.

async def save_icp_version(conn, criteria: list) -> int: cursor = await conn.execute('SELECT MAX(version) FROM icp_definition'); row = await cursor.fetchone(); next_version = (row[0] or 0) + 1; now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(); await conn.execute('INSERT INTO icp_definition (id, version, criteria, created_at) VALUES (?,?,?,?)', (str(uuid.uuid4()), next_version, json.dumps(criteria), now)); await conn.commit(); return next_version.

async def save_icp_suggestion(conn, suggestion_text: str, evidence: str) -> None: await conn.execute('INSERT INTO icp_suggestions (id, suggestion_text, evidence, status, created_at) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?)', (str(uuid.uuid4()), suggestion_text, evidence, 'pending', datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())); await conn.commit().

adela_outbound/api/main.py must NOT be modified. Instead, seed_icp_if_empty must be called from the qualification graph's startup sequence — specifically called in the input_loader node if ICP is not yet loaded. Alternatively, add a startup call in the qualification router's startup event. The cleanest approach: call it lazily inside load_icp — if no rows found, call seed_icp_if_empty first then re-query. Update load_icp: if not row: await seed_icp_if_empty(conn); cursor2 = await conn.execute('SELECT * FROM icp_definition ORDER BY version DESC LIMIT 1'); row = await cursor2.fetchone(); if not row: raise RuntimeError('ICP seeding failed').
tests/test_qualification_scorer.py — add ICP seeding tests:

async def test_seed_icp_if_empty_inserts_criteria(tmp_path): Setup temp DB with CREATE_TABLES_SQL. Call await seed_icp_if_empty(conn). Query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM icp_definition. Assert count == 1. Query criteria: SELECT criteria FROM icp_definition. Parse JSON. Assert len(criteria) == 8.
async def test_seed_icp_if_empty_is_idempotent(tmp_path): Call seed_icp_if_empty twice on same DB. Assert still only 1 row in icp_definition.

async def test_load_icp_returns_dict_with_criteria_list(tmp_path): Seed DB. Call load_icp(conn). Assert result is dict. Assert 'criteria' in result. Assert isinstance(result['criteria'], list). Assert len(result['criteria']) == 8.
python3 -m pytest tests/test_qualification_scorer.py -v -k icp — all PASSED.
userStories.notes: The lazy seeding inside load_icp (seed if empty, then re-query) is the cleanest approach because it avoids needing to modify main.py's lifespan handler. The qualification graph calls load_icp in its input_loader node, which means seeding happens automatically on the first qualification run. No separate startup hook needed. save_icp_version uses INSERT not UPDATE — every ICP change creates a new version row, preserving the full history. The dashboard reads the highest version number.

userStories.title: Per-criterion Claude scorer and aggregate score calculator
userStories.description: Implement scorer.py with per-criterion Claude scoring (one call per criterion, run concurrently) and the weighted aggregate scorer that produces fit_score and fit_tier.
acceptanceCriteria: adela_outbound/agents/qualification/scorer.py — replace stub with full implementation.
Imports: from __future__ import annotations; import logging; import json; import asyncio; from adela_outbound.config import config; logger = logging.getLogger(__name__).
WEIGHT_VALUES module-level dict: WEIGHT_VALUES = {'high': 3, 'medium': 2, 'low': 1}.
FIT_TIER_THRESHOLDS module-level dict: FIT_TIER_THRESHOLDS = {'strong': 0.75, 'moderate': 0.50, 'weak': 0.30}.

CRITERION_SCORING_PROMPT module-level string: 'You are evaluating a B2B prospect against a specific ICP criterion for Adela — a context and governance layer for services companies deploying bespoke technical work across enterprise clients. Score the prospect on a 0-3 scale: 0=no evidence this criterion is met, 1=weak or indirect signal, 2=moderate evidence, 3=strong clear evidence. Your evidence string must cite specific facts from the brief — never generic statements. Return a JSON object with exactly these keys: score (integer 0-3), evidence (string, 1-2 sentences citing specific details from the brief that justify the score), confidence (float 0.0-1.0 reflecting how certain you are given available information). Return only the JSON object.'

async def score_criterion(brief: dict, criterion: dict, client: object) -> dict: Takes a ProspectBrief dict, an ICPCriterion dict, and an AsyncAnthropic client. Returns dict with: criterion_id (str), score (int 0-3), evidence (str), confidence (float).
API call: response = await client.messages.create(model='claude-sonnet-4-20250514', max_tokens=300, system=CRITERION_SCORING_PROMPT, messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': user_msg}]).

Parse: content = response.content[0].text.strip() if response.content else ''. Strip markdown fences (same pattern as all other agents). Parse JSON. If parse fails or result not dict: return {'criterion_id': criterion['id'], 'score': 0, 'evidence': 'Scoring failed — JSON parse error', 'confidence': 0.0}.

Validate score: score = int(parsed.get('score', 0)); score = max(0, min(3, score)). Return: {'criterion_id': criterion['id'], 'score': score, 'evidence': str(parsed.get('evidence', ''))[:500], 'confidence': float(parsed.get('confidence', 0.5))}.

Outer try/except Exception as e: logger.warning(f'Criterion scoring failed for {criterion["id"]}: {e}'); return {'criterion_id': criterion['id'], 'score': 0, 'evidence': f'Scoring unavailable: {str(e)[:100]}', 'confidence': 0.0}.

async def score_all_criteria(brief: dict, icp: dict, client: object) -> list: Takes ProspectBrief dict, ICPDefinition dict (with criteria as list), and AsyncAnthropic client. Runs all criteria concurrently: criteria = icp.get('criteria', []); tasks = [score_criterion(brief, c, client) for c in criteria]; results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True). Safe unpack: scores = []; for i, r in enumerate(results): if isinstance(r, Exception): criterion_id = criteria[i]['id'] if i < len(criteria) else f'criterion_{i}'; logger.warning(f'score_criterion raised for {criterion_id}: {r}'); scores.append({'criterion_id': criterion_id, 'score': 0, 'evidence': f'Exception: {str(r)[:100]}', 'confidence': 0.0}); else: scores.append(r). Return scores.

def aggregate_scores(criterion_scores: list, icp: dict) -> dict: Pure synchronous function — no async, no IO. Builds lookup: score_by_id = {s['criterion_id']: s['score'] for s in criterion_scores}. Builds weight lookup from icp criteria: weight_by_id = {c['id']: WEIGHT_VALUES.get(c['weight'], 1) for c in icp.get('criteria', [])}. Compute weighted sum: weighted_sum = sum(score_by_id.get(cid, 0) * w for cid, w in weight_by_id.items()). Compute max possible: max_possible = sum(3 * w for w in weight_by_id.values()). fit_score = round(weighted_sum / max_possible, 3) if max_possible > 0 else 0.0. fit_tier: if fit_score >= FIT_TIER_THRESHOLDS['strong']: fit_tier = 'strong'; elif fit_score >= FIT_TIER_THRESHOLDS['moderate']: fit_tier = 'moderate'; elif fit_score >= FIT_TIER_THRESHOLDS['weak']: fit_tier = 'weak'; else: fit_tier = 'disqualified'. Return: {'fit_score': fit_score, 'fit_tier': fit_tier, 'weighted_sum': weighted_sum, 'max_possible': max_possible}.
tests/test_qualification_scorer.py — add scorer tests:

def test_aggregate_scores_strong(): icp = {'criteria': [{'id': 'c1', 'weight': 'high'}, {'id': 'c2', 'weight': 'medium'}]}; scores = [{'criterion_id': 'c1', 'score': 3}, {'criterion_id': 'c2', 'score': 3}]; result = aggregate_scores(scores, icp); assert result['fit_tier'] == 'strong'; assert result['fit_score'] >= 0.75.

def test_aggregate_scores_disqualified(): icp = {'criteria': [{'id': 'c1', 'weight': 'high'}]}; scores = [{'criterion_id': 'c1', 'score': 0}]; result = aggregate_scores(scores, icp); assert result['fit_tier'] == 'disqualified'; assert result['fit_score'] == 0.0.

def test_aggregate_scores_handles_missing_criterion(): icp = {'criteria': [{'id': 'c1', 'weight': 'high'}, {'id': 'c2', 'weight': 'low'}]}; scores = [{'criterion_id': 'c1', 'score': 3}]; result = aggregate_scores(scores, icp); assert 0.0 <= result['fit_score'] <= 1.0.

async def test_score_criterion_returns_zero_on_claude_failure(tmp_path): Mock AsyncAnthropic client with messages.create raising Exception('API error'). Call result = await score_criterion({'summary': 'test'}, {'id': 'test_crit', 'name': 'Test', 'description': 'test', 'weight': 'high'}, mock_client). Assert result['criterion_id'] == 'test_crit'. Assert result['score'] == 0.
python3 -m pytest tests/test_qualification_scorer.py -v — all PASSED.
userStories.notes: score_all_criteria runs all 8 criterion scorers concurrently — that is 8 simultaneous Claude API calls. This is intentional: sequential scoring would take 8x longer. The API cost is acceptable since qualification is a HITL-gated step that only runs after a human-approved discovery process. The aggregate_scores function is synchronous because it does pure arithmetic — no IO needed. Keeping it sync makes it trivially testable.

userStories.title: QualificationState, nodes, and LangGraph graph with MemorySaver HITL
userStories.description: Implement the complete QualificationState, all six nodes, and the LangGraph StateGraph with MemorySaver checkpointer and interrupt_before=['hitl_gate']. This is the most critical story — the HITL pattern must be exactly right.
acceptanceCriteria: adela_outbound/agents/qualification/state.py — replace stub: from __future__ import annotations; from typing import TypedDict, Optional; class QualificationState(TypedDict): company_id: str; prospect_brief: dict; icp_definition: dict; criterion_scores: list; qualification_brief: Optional[dict]; decision: Optional[str]; rejection_note: Optional[str]; errors: list.
adela_outbound/agents/qualification/nodes.py — replace stub with full implementation.

nodes.py imports: from __future__ import annotations; import logging; import json; import uuid; from datetime import datetime, timezone; from typing import Optional; from anthropic import AsyncAnthropic; from adela_outbound.agents.qualification.state import QualificationState; from adela_outbound.agents.qualification.scorer import score_all_criteria, aggregate_scores; from adela_outbound.agents.qualification.icp import load_icp; from adela_outbound.agents.qualification.events import broadcast; from adela_outbound.db.connection import get_db; from adela_outbound.config import config; logger = logging.getLogger(__name__).

input_loader node: async def input_loader(state: QualificationState) -> dict. Loads prospect brief: async with get_db() as conn: cursor = await conn.execute('SELECT pb.*, dq.company_name, dq.website, dq.github_handle, dq.twitter_handle, dq.discovery_signal FROM prospect_briefs pb JOIN discovery_queue dq ON pb.company_id = dq.id WHERE pb.company_id = ?', [state['company_id']]); row = await cursor.fetchone(); if not row: raise ValueError(f'No prospect brief found for company_id={state["company_id"]}. Research must complete before qualification.'). Parse brief: d = dict(row); d['pain_points'] = json.loads(d.get('pain_points') or '[]'); d['personalization_hooks'] = json.loads(d.get('personalization_hooks') or '[]'); d['research_sources'] = json.loads(d.get('research_sources') or '[]'); d['creative_outreach_opportunity'] = bool(d.get('creative_outreach_opportunity', 0)). Load ICP: icp = await load_icp(conn). Return: {'prospect_brief': d, 'icp_definition': icp, 'errors': []}.

criterion_scorer node: async def criterion_scorer(state: QualificationState) -> dict. client = AsyncAnthropic(api_key=config.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). scores = await score_all_criteria(state['prospect_brief'], state['icp_definition'], client). Return: {'criterion_scores': scores}.

aggregate_scorer node: async def aggregate_scorer(state: QualificationState) -> dict. result = aggregate_scores(state['criterion_scores'], state['icp_definition']). Return: {'qualification_brief': {**result, 'criterion_scores': state['criterion_scores']}}.

qualification_brief_builder node: async def qualification_brief_builder(state: QualificationState) -> dict. brief = dict(state.get('qualification_brief') or {}). Build why_now and suggested_outreach_angle with a single Claude call. client = AsyncAnthropic(api_key=config.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). prompt = f'Given this ICP qualification result for company "{state["prospect_brief"].get("company_name", "")}": fit_score={brief.get("fit_score", 0)}, fit_tier={brief.get("fit_tier", "")}, top criterion scores: {json.dumps(state["criterion_scores"][:3])}. In 1-2 sentences each, provide: (1) why_now: why is now the right time to reach out to this company specifically? (2) suggested_outreach_angle: what specific angle or hook should the outreach message lead with? Return JSON with keys why_now and suggested_outreach_angle only.'. try: response = await client.messages.create(model='claude-sonnet-4-20250514', max_tokens=200, messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': prompt}]); content = response.content[0].text.strip() if response.content else '{}'; strip fences; parsed = json.loads(content); brief['why_now'] = parsed.get('why_now', ''); brief['suggested_outreach_angle'] = parsed.get('suggested_outreach_angle', ''); except Exception as e: logger.warning(f'why_now generation failed: {e}'); brief['why_now'] = f'Score: {brief.get("fit_score", 0)} ({brief.get("fit_tier", "")})'; brief['suggested_outreach_angle'] = 'Review criterion scores for outreach angle'.

Auto-disqualify logic inside qualification_brief_builder: if brief.get('fit_tier') == 'disqualified': brief['status'] = 'auto_rejected'; brief['id'] = str(uuid.uuid4()); brief['company_id'] = state['company_id']; brief['created_at'] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(). Write to DB and update discovery_queue: async with get_db() as conn: await conn.execute('INSERT OR REPLACE INTO qualification_briefs (id, company_id, fit_score, fit_tier, criterion_scores, why_now, suggested_outreach_angle, status, rejection_note, reviewed_at, created_at) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)', (brief['id'], brief['company_id'], brief.get('fit_score',0), brief.get('fit_tier',''), json.dumps(state['criterion_scores']), brief.get('why_now',''), brief.get('suggested_outreach_angle',''), 'auto_rejected', 'Auto-rejected: fit_score below 0.30 threshold', None, brief['created_at'])); await conn.execute('UPDATE discovery_queue SET status = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?', ['disqualified', datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(), state['company_id']]); await conn.commit(). Return: {'qualification_brief': brief, 'decision': 'auto_rejected'}.

For non-disqualified: set brief['status'] = 'pending_review'; brief['id'] = str(uuid.uuid4()); brief['company_id'] = state['company_id']; brief['created_at'] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(). Write to DB with status='pending_review'. Emit SSE: await broadcast('qualify_pending', {'company_id': state['company_id'], 'company_name': state['prospect_brief'].get('company_name',''), 'fit_score': brief.get('fit_score',0), 'fit_tier': brief.get('fit_tier',''), 'timestamp': datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()}). Return: {'qualification_brief': brief}.

hitl_gate node: async def hitl_gate(state: QualificationState) -> dict. This node does nothing — it is only a pause point. The interrupt happens BEFORE this node executes (interrupt_before=['hitl_gate']). The node body: logger.info(f'Resuming hitl_gate for {state["company_id"]} with decision={state.get("decision")}'); return {}.

resume_handler node: async def resume_handler(state: QualificationState) -> dict. decision = state.get('decision', ''); rejection_note = state.get('rejection_note'); now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(); brief_id = state.get('qualification_brief', {}).get('id', ''). If decision == 'approved': async with get_db() as conn: await conn.execute('UPDATE qualification_briefs SET status = ?, reviewed_at = ? WHERE id = ?', ['approved', now, brief_id]); await conn.execute('UPDATE discovery_queue SET status = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?', ['qualified', now, state['company_id']]); await conn.commit(); logger.info(f'Approved: {state["company_id"]}'). Elif decision == 'rejected': async with get_db() as conn: await conn.execute('UPDATE qualification_briefs SET status = ?, rejection_note = ?, reviewed_at = ? WHERE id = ?', ['rejected', rejection_note, now, brief_id]); await conn.execute('UPDATE discovery_queue SET status = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?', ['disqualified', now, state['company_id']]); await conn.execute('INSERT INTO icp_feedback (id, company_id, decision, rejection_note, decided_at) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?)', (str(uuid.uuid4()), state['company_id'], 'rejected', rejection_note, now)); await conn.commit(). Return: {'errors': state.get('errors', [])}.
python3 -c 'from adela_outbound.agents.qualification.nodes import input_loader, criterion_scorer, aggregate_scorer, qualification_brief_builder, hitl_gate, resume_handler; print("OK")' exits 0.
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v — all PASSED.
userStories.notes: The hitl_gate node is intentionally empty. LangGraph's interrupt_before=['hitl_gate'] pauses the graph BEFORE the node executes. When the graph is resumed, hitl_gate runs (does nothing) then the edge to resume_handler fires. This is correct — do not put logic in hitl_gate. The auto-disqualify logic inside qualification_brief_builder short-circuits the HITL flow by setting decision='auto_rejected' and writing directly to DB. The graph still routes through hitl_gate and resume_handler, but resume_handler sees decision='auto_rejected' — add an elif branch in resume_handler for 'auto_rejected' that simply returns without doing anything (the DB was already written).

userStories.title: LangGraph graph assembly with MemorySaver, run_qualification, and resume_qualification
userStories.description: Assemble the StateGraph with MemorySaver checkpointer, implement run_qualification() and resume_qualification() functions, and write a conditional edge for auto-disqualified companies.
acceptanceCriteria: adela_outbound/agents/qualification/graph.py — replace stub with full implementation.

Imports: from __future__ import annotations; import uuid; from datetime import datetime, timezone; from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, END; from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver; from langgraph.types import Command; from adela_outbound.agents.qualification.state import QualificationState; from adela_outbound.agents.qualification.nodes import input_loader, criterion_scorer, aggregate_scorer, qualification_brief_builder, hitl_gate, resume_handler.

Checkpointer at module level: checkpointer = MemorySaver(). This is a module-level singleton — it persists graph state across the run_qualification and resume_qualification calls within the same process lifetime.

Graph with conditional edge for auto-disqualified: builder = StateGraph(QualificationState); builder.add_node('input_loader', input_loader); builder.add_node('criterion_scorer', criterion_scorer); builder.add_node('aggregate_scorer', aggregate_scorer); builder.add_node('qualification_brief_builder', qualification_brief_builder); builder.add_node('hitl_gate', hitl_gate); builder.add_node('resume_handler', resume_handler); builder.set_entry_point('input_loader'); builder.add_edge('input_loader', 'criterion_scorer'); builder.add_edge('criterion_scorer', 'aggregate_scorer'); builder.add_edge('aggregate_scorer', 'qualification_brief_builder').

Conditional edge after qualification_brief_builder: def _route_after_brief(state: QualificationState) -> str: if state.get('decision') == 'auto_rejected': return 'resume_handler'; return 'hitl_gate'. builder.add_conditional_edges('qualification_brief_builder', _route_after_brief, {'resume_handler': 'resume_handler', 'hitl_gate': 'hitl_gate'}); builder.add_edge('hitl_gate', 'resume_handler'); builder.add_edge('resume_handler', END).
Compile with interrupt and checkpointer: qualification_graph = builder.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer, interrupt_before=['hitl_gate']).

run_qualification function: async def run_qualification(company_id: str) -> dict: config = {'configurable': {'thread_id': company_id}}; initial: QualificationState = {'company_id': company_id, 'prospect_brief': {}, 'icp_definition': {}, 'criterion_scores': [], 'qualification_brief': None, 'decision': None, 'rejection_note': None, 'errors': []}; result = await qualification_graph.ainvoke(initial, config=config); return result. Note: when the graph hits interrupt_before=['hitl_gate'], ainvoke returns the current state — it does NOT raise an exception. The caller receives the state dict with decision=None.

resume_qualification function: async def resume_qualification(company_id: str, decision: str, rejection_note: str = None) -> dict: config = {'configurable': {'thread_id': company_id}}; resume_data = {'decision': decision, 'rejection_note': rejection_note}; result = await qualification_graph.ainvoke(Command(resume=resume_data), config=config); return result. The Command(resume=...) payload is merged into the graph state when the graph resumes. After resume, hitl_gate executes (does nothing), then resume_handler executes with the updated state.
python3 -c 'from adela_outbound.agents.qualification.graph import qualification_graph, run_qualification, resume_qualification, checkpointer; print("OK")' exits 0.
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v — all PASSED.
userStories.notes: The MemorySaver checkpointer stores graph state in memory — it is NOT persisted to disk. If the server restarts, all paused graphs are lost. This is acceptable for MVP since we do not expect the server to restart while Justin is in the middle of reviewing briefs. For production, replace MemorySaver with a SQLite-backed or Redis-backed checkpointer. The thread_id=company_id pattern means each company has exactly one graph checkpoint. If run_qualification is called twice for the same company_id, the second call overwrites the first checkpoint — this is intentional (re-running research replaces the old qualification).

userStories.title: HITL resume/reject API endpoints with mandatory rejection note enforcement
userStories.description: Implement the HITL endpoints for qualifying and rejecting briefs. Rejection notes are mandatory — the API returns 400 if the note is missing or empty. These are the most important endpoints in the system.
acceptanceCriteria: adela_outbound/api/routers/qualification.py — replace stub with full implementation.

Imports: from __future__ import annotations; from fastapi import APIRouter, BackgroundTasks, HTTPException, Request; from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse; from pydantic import BaseModel; from typing import Optional; import json; import asyncio; from datetime import datetime, timezone; from adela_outbound.db.connection import get_db; from adela_outbound.agents.qualification import events as qual_events; router = APIRouter().

Request models: class ApproveRequest(BaseModel): pass. (No body required for approve.) class RejectRequest(BaseModel): note: str; class RejectRequest validator: @validator('note') def note_must_not_be_empty(cls, v): if not v or not v.strip(): raise ValueError('Rejection note is required and cannot be empty'); return v.strip(). Use pydantic v2 syntax: from pydantic import field_validator; @field_validator('note') @classmethod def note_not_empty(cls, v: str) -> str: if not v or not v.strip(): raise ValueError('Rejection note required'); return v.strip().

POST /run/{company_id} — trigger qualification: @router.post('/run/{company_id}') async def trigger_qualification(company_id: str, background_tasks: BackgroundTasks) -> dict: from adela_outbound.agents.qualification.graph import run_qualification; background_tasks.add_task(run_qualification, company_id); return {'status': 'started', 'company_id': company_id}. Import inside function body.

POST /approve/{company_id}: @router.post('/approve/{company_id}') async def approve_qualification(company_id: str) -> dict. Check graph is paused: from adela_outbound.agents.qualification.graph import qualification_graph, checkpointer; config = {'configurable': {'thread_id': company_id}}; state = qualification_graph.get_state(config); if not state or not state.next: raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f'No paused qualification found for company_id={company_id}. Either the graph has not run yet or already completed.'). Resume: from adela_outbound.agents.qualification.graph import resume_qualification; result = await resume_qualification(company_id, decision='approved'); return {'status': 'approved', 'company_id': company_id}.

POST /reject/{company_id}: @router.post('/reject/{company_id}') async def reject_qualification(company_id: str, body: RejectRequest) -> dict. Pydantic validates the note is non-empty — if empty, FastAPI returns 422 automatically. Additionally check manually: if not body.note.strip(): raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail='Rejection note is required'). Check graph is paused (same as approve). Resume: result = await resume_qualification(company_id, decision='rejected', rejection_note=body.note.strip()); return {'status': 'rejected', 'company_id': company_id, 'note_recorded': True}.

GET /queue — MUST be defined before GET /{company_id}/brief: @router.get('/queue') async def get_qualification_queue(limit: int = 50) -> list. Queries qualification_briefs JOIN discovery_queue: async with get_db() as conn: cursor = await conn.execute('SELECT qb.id, qb.company_id, dq.company_name, qb.fit_score, qb.fit_tier, qb.why_now, qb.suggested_outreach_angle, qb.status, qb.created_at FROM qualification_briefs qb JOIN discovery_queue dq ON qb.company_id = dq.id WHERE qb.status = ? ORDER BY qb.fit_score DESC LIMIT ?', ['pending_review', limit]); rows = await cursor.fetchall(); return [dict(r) for r in rows].

GET /{company_id}/brief: @router.get('/{company_id}/brief') async def get_qualification_brief(company_id: str) -> dict. Queries qualification_briefs JOIN discovery_queue for company_name and prospect_briefs for brief details: async with get_db() as conn: cursor = await conn.execute('SELECT qb.*, dq.company_name, dq.website FROM qualification_briefs qb JOIN discovery_queue dq ON qb.company_id = dq.id WHERE qb.company_id = ?', [company_id]); row = await cursor.fetchone(); if not row: raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f'No qualification brief for {company_id}'). d = dict(row); d['criterion_scores'] = json.loads(d.get('criterion_scores') or '[]'). Also fetch prospect_brief: cursor2 = await conn.execute('SELECT summary, adela_relevance, personalization_hooks, recommended_channel, creative_outreach_opportunity FROM prospect_briefs WHERE company_id = ?', [company_id]); brief_row = await cursor2.fetchone(); d['prospect_brief_summary'] = dict(brief_row) if brief_row else {}. Return d.

GET /stream — SSE endpoint same pattern as discovery and research: @router.get('/stream') async def stream_qualify_events(request: Request) -> StreamingResponse. Exact same implementation as research branch /stream endpoint but using qual_events.sse_queues.
python3 -c 'from adela_outbound.api.routers.qualification import router; print(len(router.routes), "routes")' exits 0.
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v — all PASSED.
userStories.notes: The graph.get_state(config) call checks if a checkpoint exists for the thread_id. state.next is a tuple of node names that will execute next — if the graph is paused at interrupt_before=['hitl_gate'], state.next == ('hitl_gate',). If state.next is empty or None, the graph has either not run or already completed. The 404 check prevents resume_qualification from being called on a non-paused graph, which would cause LangGraph to raise an error. Route ordering: GET /queue before GET /{company_id}/brief.

userStories.title: ICP management endpoints — read, update, suggestions
userStories.description: Implement the ICP management endpoints that allow the dashboard to read the current ICP, save new versions, and manage refinement suggestions.
acceptanceCriteria: adela_outbound/api/routers/qualification.py — add ICP endpoints.

GET /icp: @router.get('/icp') async def get_icp() -> dict. async with get_db() as conn: icp = await load_icp(conn). Return icp dict. Import load_icp at top of file: from adela_outbound.agents.qualification.icp import load_icp, save_icp_version, save_icp_suggestion.

PUT /icp: @router.put('/icp') async def update_icp(body: dict) -> dict. Validate body has 'criteria' key as list: if 'criteria' not in body or not isinstance(body['criteria'], list): raise HTTPException(400, 'Body must have criteria as list'). Validate each criterion has id, name, description, weight: for c in body['criteria']: if not all(k in c for k in ['id', 'name', 'description', 'weight']): raise HTTPException(400, f'Criterion missing required fields: {c}'). Save: async with get_db() as conn: new_version = await save_icp_version(conn, body['criteria']). Return: {'status': 'saved', 'version': new_version, 'criteria_count': len(body['criteria'])}.

GET /icp/suggestions: @router.get('/icp/suggestions') async def get_icp_suggestions() -> list. async with get_db() as conn: cursor = await conn.execute('SELECT * FROM icp_suggestions WHERE status = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC', ['pending']); rows = await cursor.fetchall(); return [dict(r) for r in rows].

POST /icp/suggestions/{suggestion_id}/accept: @router.post('/icp/suggestions/{suggestion_id}/accept') async def accept_suggestion(suggestion_id: str) -> dict. Fetch suggestion: async with get_db() as conn: cursor = await conn.execute('SELECT * FROM icp_suggestions WHERE id = ?', [suggestion_id]); row = await cursor.fetchone(); if not row: raise HTTPException(404, 'Suggestion not found'). The suggestion_text contains the full new ICP criteria as JSON — parse it: try: new_criteria = json.loads(dict(row).get('suggestion_text', '[]')); except: raise HTTPException(400, 'Could not parse suggestion as criteria JSON'). Save new version: new_version = await save_icp_version(conn, new_criteria). Mark accepted: await conn.execute('UPDATE icp_suggestions SET status = ? WHERE id = ?', ['accepted', suggestion_id]); await conn.commit(). Return: {'status': 'accepted', 'new_version': new_version}.

POST /icp/suggestions/{suggestion_id}/reject: @router.post('/icp/suggestions/{suggestion_id}/reject') async def reject_suggestion(suggestion_id: str) -> dict. async with get_db() as conn: result = await conn.execute('UPDATE icp_suggestions SET status = ? WHERE id = ?', ['rejected', suggestion_id]); await conn.commit(); if result.rowcount == 0: raise HTTPException(404, 'Suggestion not found'). Return: {'status': 'rejected'}.
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v — all PASSED.
userStories.notes: The accept_suggestion endpoint assumes suggestion_text contains JSON-serialised criteria list. This is enforced when suggestions are created by the weekly batch job in US-008. The suggestion format must be documented clearly so the dashboard knows how to display suggestions (as text for human reading) while the backend parses them as criteria JSON for acceptance.

userStories.title: Weekly ICP refinement suggestion job
userStories.description: Implement the APScheduler job that runs weekly, analyses recent rejection patterns using Claude, and saves up to 3 ICP refinement suggestions to the icp_suggestions table.
acceptanceCriteria: adela_outbound/agents/qualification/icp.py — add weekly suggestion generation function.

async def generate_icp_suggestions() -> None: Module-level async function with no parameters. Opens DB connection. Fetches last 30 days of icp_feedback: async with get_db() as conn: cursor = await conn.execute('SELECT f.company_id, f.decision, f.rejection_note, dq.company_name, dq.discovery_signal FROM icp_feedback f JOIN discovery_queue dq ON f.company_id = dq.id WHERE DATE(f.decided_at) >= DATE(\'now\', \'-30 days\') ORDER BY f.decided_at DESC'); rows = await cursor.fetchall(); feedback_list = [dict(r) for r in rows].
If len(feedback_list) < 3: logger.info('Not enough feedback data for ICP suggestions (need >= 3 rejections)'); return.

Claude call: from anthropic import AsyncAnthropic; client = AsyncAnthropic(api_key=config.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY); current_icp = await load_icp(conn); icp_str = json.dumps(current_icp.get('criteria', [])). system = f'Current ICP criteria for context: {icp_str}'. response = await client.messages.create(model='claude-sonnet-4-20250514', max_tokens=1500, system=system, messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': prompt}]).

Parse response: content = response.content[0].text.strip() if response.content else '[]'; strip fences; try: suggestions = json.loads(content); except: logger.warning('ICP suggestion generation returned invalid JSON'); return. if not isinstance(suggestions, list): return.

Save each suggestion: for s in suggestions[:3]: if isinstance(s, dict) and s.get('suggestion_text') and s.get('new_criteria'): await save_icp_suggestion(conn, json.dumps(s.get('new_criteria', [])), s.get('evidence', '')).

adela_outbound/scheduler.py must NOT be modified — it is owned by feature/agent-discovery. Instead, the qualification router registers the weekly job on startup using a FastAPI startup event on the router itself. Add to qualification.py router: from fastapi import APIRouter; router = APIRouter(on_startup=[_register_weekly_job]). Define: async def _register_weekly_job() -> None: from apscheduler.schedulers.asyncio import AsyncIOScheduler; from adela_outbound.agents.qualification.icp import generate_icp_suggestions; from adela_outbound.scheduler import scheduler; scheduler.add_job(generate_icp_suggestions, 'cron', day_of_week='sun', hour=20, minute=0, id='icp_suggestion_job', replace_existing=True, misfire_grace_time=3600); logger.info('Registered weekly ICP suggestion job'). This attaches to the existing scheduler singleton from scheduler.py without modifying that file.
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v — all PASSED.
userStories.notes: The on_startup callback on the router is a FastAPI feature — it fires when the application starts, just like lifespan handlers. This is the correct way for this branch to add to the scheduler without modifying scheduler.py (which is owned by feature/agent-discovery). The scheduler singleton is imported from adela_outbound.scheduler — it is the same object that main.py started in the lifespan handler. Adding a job to it after startup works because APScheduler supports adding jobs at any time while running.

userStories.title: Integration test — full graph run with HITL pause and resume
userStories.description: Write an integration test that runs the full qualification graph, verifies it pauses at the HITL interrupt, then resumes with an approval decision and verifies the SQLite output.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/test_qualification_graph.py — replace placeholder with full integration test.

Setup function: create temp SQLite DB, run CREATE_TABLES_SQL, insert test data. Insert into discovery_queue: id='qual-test-001', company_name='FDE Tools', status='researched' (and all other required columns). Insert into prospect_briefs: company_id='qual-test-001', summary='FDE Tools builds forward deployed engineering tooling for enterprise clients scaling across multiple regulated industries.', current_focus='Scaling from 3 to 12 enterprise fintech clients.', pain_points=json.dumps(['Context fragmentation across client deployments', 'No SOP versioning for agent configurations']), adela_relevance='Direct ICP match — scaling regulated enterprise deployments with no governance tooling.', personalization_hooks=json.dumps(['Open GitHub issue about multi-tenant context isolation']), creative_outreach_opportunity=1, creative_outreach_detail='GitHub issue #5', recommended_channel='github', research_sources=json.dumps(['https://fdetools.io']), confidence_score=0.8, raw_research='{}', created_at=now. Insert into icp_definition with the 8 seed criteria.
Patch config.DB_PATH in connection module and nodes module. Patch config.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = 'test'.

Mock Claude for criterion scoring: patch score_all_criteria to return 8 criterion score dicts each with score=2, evidence='Strong signal', confidence=0.8. Mock AsyncAnthropic for qualification_brief_builder why_now generation to return JSON with why_now and suggested_outreach_angle.

Test HITL pause: from adela_outbound.agents.qualification.graph import run_qualification, resume_qualification, qualification_graph, checkpointer; result = await run_qualification('qual-test-001'). Assert result is not None (graph returned state at interrupt). Verify graph is paused: from langgraph.types import StateSnapshot; state = qualification_graph.get_state({'configurable': {'thread_id': 'qual-test-001'}}); assert state is not None; assert 'hitl_gate' in (state.next or ()) or state.next, f'Expected graph to be paused at hitl_gate, got next={state.next}'.

Verify brief was written to DB before pause: async with aiosqlite.connect(db_path) as conn: cursor = await conn.execute('SELECT status FROM qualification_briefs WHERE company_id = ?', ['qual-test-001']); row = await cursor.fetchone(); assert row is not None; assert row[0] == 'pending_review'.

Test resume with approval: result2 = await resume_qualification('qual-test-001', decision='approved'). Verify DB updated: async with aiosqlite.connect(db_path) as conn: cursor = await conn.execute('SELECT status FROM qualification_briefs WHERE company_id = ?', ['qual-test-001']); row = await cursor.fetchone(); assert row[0] == 'approved'. cursor2 = await conn.execute('SELECT status FROM discovery_queue WHERE id = ?', ['qual-test-001']); row2 = await cursor2.fetchone(); assert row2[0] == 'qualified'.

Test auto-disqualify (separate test): mock score_all_criteria to return all scores of 0. Run run_qualification for a new company_id 'qual-test-002'. Verify: qualification_briefs status = 'auto_rejected'. Verify discovery_queue status = 'disqualified'. Verify graph is NOT paused (state.next is empty).
python3 -m pytest tests/test_qualification_graph.py -v — all PASSED.
userStories.notes: The HITL pause verification using qualification_graph.get_state() is the key assertion. state.next should contain 'hitl_gate' when paused. However, LangGraph's exact API for get_state varies slightly by version — if state.next is a tuple, check 'hitl_gate' in state.next. If it is a list, check similarly. The assertion uses 'hitl_gate' in (state.next or ()) which handles both tuple and list. The test imports qualification_graph directly from graph.py — the same module-level compiled graph that the API endpoints use. This means the test's paused graph state is accessible to the resume_qualification call because they share the same in-memory checkpointer.

userStories.title: events.py SSE bus and README
userStories.description: Replace events.py stub with full implementation and write project documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: adela_outbound/agents/qualification/events.py — replace stub: import asyncio; sse_queues: list = []; async def broadcast(event: str, data: dict) -> None: for q in sse_queues: try: q.put_nowait({'event': event, 'data': data}); except asyncio.QueueFull: pass. Zero imports from adela_outbound.
.env.example: confirm ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is present (from cherry-pick). No new API keys needed by qualification agent.

README.md — create/update: ## Adela Outbound — ICP Qualification Agent. ## What This Agent Does. ## HITL Architecture (explains interrupt/resume pattern, why every decision must be manual). ## Parallel Branch Architecture (cherry-pick requirement, locked files warning). ## Setup. ## Running (port 8002 to avoid conflict with discovery 8000 and research 8001). ## API Endpoints table: POST /run/{id}, POST /approve/{id}, POST /reject/{id} (note: rejection note required), GET /queue, GET /{id}/brief, GET /icp, PUT /icp, GET /icp/suggestions, POST /icp/suggestions/{id}/accept, POST /icp/suggestions/{id}/reject, GET /stream. ## Testing. ## Shared Contract Warning.

README.md note on rejection: '> ⚠️ **Rejection notes are mandatory.** POST /reject/{company_id} returns HTTP 422 if the note field is missing or empty. This is enforced at the API layer and is intentional — rejection notes feed the weekly ICP refinement loop.'
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v — all PASSED.
python3 -c 'from adela_outbound.api.routers.qualification import router; print("OK")' exits 0.
userStories.notes: Port 8002 for the qualification agent during development avoids conflicts with discovery (8000) and research (8001). In the merged service, all three share port 8000 under different route prefixes.

constraints.python39Compat: Python 3.9.6. from __future__ import annotations at top of all files with type hints. Optional[X] not X|None. No match/case.
constraints.fileOwnership: Only files in parallelContext.ownedFiles. Never touch doNotTouch.
constraints.noModifyMainPy: adela_outbound/api/main.py is owned by feature/agent-discovery. Do not modify it. Use router on_startup for the weekly job registration.
constraints.noModifySchedulerPy: adela_outbound/scheduler.py is owned by feature/agent-discovery. Add the weekly job via the router on_startup callback which calls scheduler.add_job() on the existing singleton.
constraints.hitlIsMandatory: Every qualification decision (approved or rejected) must come from Justin via the API. The graph must NEVER auto-approve any company regardless of fit_score. Auto-rejection for fit_score < 0.30 is the only exception and must be clearly labelled as 'auto_rejected' not 'approved'.
constraints.rejectionNoteRequired: POST /reject/{company_id} must return HTTP 400 or 422 if rejection note is missing or empty string. This is a hard requirement — do not make it optional.
constraints.memoryCheckpointerScope: MemorySaver stores state in memory. All graph state is lost on server restart. This is acceptable for MVP. The module-level checkpointer singleton means run_qualification and resume_qualification share the same checkpoint store within one process.
constraints.commandResumeImport: from langgraph.types import Command — confirm this import path works for your LangGraph version. If it fails, try: from langgraph import Command. Run python3 -c 'from langgraph.types import Command; print("OK")' to verify before using it.
constraints.routeOrdering: GET /queue before GET /{company_id}/brief. GET /icp/suggestions before GET /icp (if parameterised). Static paths always before parameterised paths.
constraints.noExternalSourceAdapters: Qualification agent has no external API source adapters (no Brave, Firecrawl, Perplexity). Only Claude for scoring.

afterEveryStory: python3 -c 'from adela_outbound.api.main import app; print("OK")' — must exit 0
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v — all collected tests must show PASSED
python3 -c 'from adela_outbound.agents.qualification.graph import qualification_graph, run_qualification, resume_qualification; print("OK")' — must exit 0 from US-005 onward
python3 -c 'from langgraph.types import Command; from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver; print("OK")' — verify LangGraph HITL imports work
